-- Hoogle documentation, generated by Haddock
-- See Hoogle, http://www.haskell.org/hoogle/


-- | Lightweight library for waiting on networked services to become available.
--   
--   Please see the README on GitHub at
--   <a>https://github.com/mbg/network-wait#readme</a> and Haddock
--   documentation for all modules, including those that are gated behind
--   package flags, at <a>https://mbg.github.io/network-wait/</a>
@package network-wait
@version 0.2.0.0


-- | This module contains computations which wait for some networked
--   service to become available, subject to some retry policy from
--   <a>Control.Retry</a>. The <a>waitSocketWith</a> function is the most
--   general function exported by this module, but several variants exist
--   for convenience. You may wish to start out with e.g. <a>waitTcp</a> or
--   <a>waitSocket</a> initially and move on to the more feature-rich
--   variants if you need their functionality.
module Network.Wait

-- | <a>waitTcp</a> <tt>retryPolicy hostName serviceName</tt> is a variant
--   of <a>waitTcpWith</a> which does not install any additional handlers.
--   
--   <pre>
--   waitTcp retryPolicyDefault "localhost" "80"
--   </pre>
waitTcp :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => RetryPolicyM m -> HostName -> ServiceName -> m Socket

-- | <a>waitTcpVerbose</a> <tt>outputHandler retryPolicy addrInfo</tt> is a
--   variant of <a>waitTcpVerboseFormat</a> which catches all exceptions
--   derived from <a>SomeException</a> and formats retry attempt
--   information using <a>defaultLogMsg</a> before passing the resulting
--   <a>String</a> to <tt>out</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   waitTcpVerbose putStrLn retryPolicyDefault "localhost" "80"
--   </pre>
waitTcpVerbose :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => (String -> m ()) -> RetryPolicyM m -> HostName -> ServiceName -> m Socket

-- | <a>waitTcpVerboseFormat</a> <tt>outputHandler retryPolicy
--   addrInfo</tt> is a variant of <a>waitTcpWith</a> which installs an
--   extra handler based on <a>logRetries</a> which passes status
--   information for each retry attempt to <tt>outputHandler</tt>.
--   
--   <pre>
--   waitTcpVerboseFormat @SomeException
--        (\b ex st -&gt; putStrLn $ defaultLogMsg b ex st)
--        retryPolicyDefault "localhost" "80"
--   </pre>
waitTcpVerboseFormat :: forall e m. (MonadIO m, MonadMask m, Exception e) => (Bool -> e -> RetryStatus -> m ()) -> RetryPolicyM m -> HostName -> ServiceName -> m Socket

-- | <a>waitTcpWith</a> <tt>extraHandlers retryPolicy hostName
--   serviceName</tt> is a variant of <a>waitSocketWith</a> which
--   constructs a suitable <a>AddrInfo</a> value for a TCP socket from
--   <tt>hostName</tt> and <tt>serviceName</tt>.
waitTcpWith :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => [RetryStatus -> Handler m Bool] -> RetryPolicyM m -> HostName -> ServiceName -> m Socket

-- | <a>waitSocket</a> <tt>retryPolicy addrInfo</tt> is a variant of
--   <a>waitSocketWith</a> which does not install any additional exception
--   handlers.
waitSocket :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => RetryPolicyM m -> AddrInfo -> m Socket

-- | <a>waitSocketVerbose</a> <tt>outputHandler retryPolicy addrInfo</tt>
--   is a variant of <a>waitSocketVerboseFormat</a> which catches all
--   exceptions derived from <a>SomeException</a> and formats retry attempt
--   information using <a>defaultLogMsg</a> before passing the resulting
--   <a>String</a> to <tt>out</tt>.
waitSocketVerbose :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => (String -> m ()) -> RetryPolicyM m -> AddrInfo -> m Socket

-- | <a>waitSocketVerboseFormat</a> <tt>outputHandler retryPolicy
--   addrInfo</tt> is a variant of <a>waitSocketWith</a> which installs an
--   extra handler based on <a>logRetries</a> which passes status
--   information for each retry attempt to <tt>outputHandler</tt>.
waitSocketVerboseFormat :: forall e m. (MonadIO m, MonadMask m, Exception e) => (Bool -> e -> RetryStatus -> m ()) -> RetryPolicyM m -> AddrInfo -> m Socket

-- | <a>waitSocketWith</a> <tt>extraHandlers retryPolicy addrInfo</tt> will
--   attempt to connect to <tt>addrInfo</tt>. If the connection fails,
--   <tt>retryPolicy</tt> is used to determine whether (and how often) this
--   function should attempt to retry establishing the connection. By
--   default, this function will retry after all exceptions (except for
--   those given by <a>skipAsyncExceptions</a>). This behaviour may be
--   customised with <tt>extraHandlers</tt> which are installed after
--   <a>skipAsyncExceptions</a>, but before the default exception handler.
--   The <tt>extraHandlers</tt> may also be used to report retry attempts
--   to e.g. the standard output or a logger.
waitSocketWith :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => [RetryStatus -> Handler m Bool] -> RetryPolicyM m -> AddrInfo -> m Socket

-- | <a>recoveringWith</a> <tt>extraHandlers retryPolicy action</tt> will
--   attempt to run <tt>action</tt>. If the <tt>action</tt> fails,
--   <tt>retryPolicy</tt> is used to determine whether (and how often) this
--   function should attempt to retry <tt>action</tt>. By default, this
--   function will retry after all exceptions (except for those given by
--   <a>skipAsyncExceptions</a>). This behaviour may be customised with
--   <tt>extraHandlers</tt> which are installed after
--   <a>skipAsyncExceptions</a>, but before the default exception handler.
--   The <tt>extraHandlers</tt> may also be used to report retry attempts
--   to e.g. the standard output or a logger.
recoveringWith :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => [RetryStatus -> Handler m Bool] -> RetryPolicyM m -> m a -> m a


-- | This module exports variants of the functions from <a>Network.Wait</a>
--   specialised for PostgreSQL servers. In addition to checking whether a
--   connection can be established, the functions in this module also check
--   whether the PostgreSQL server is ready to accept commands.
module Network.Wait.PostgreSQL

-- | <a>waitPostgreSql</a> <tt>retryPolicy connectInfo</tt> is a variant of
--   <tt>waitPostgresWith</tt> which does not install any additional
--   handlers.
waitPostgreSql :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => RetryPolicyM m -> ConnectInfo -> m Connection

-- | <a>waitPostgreSqlVerbose</a> <tt>outputHandler retryPolicy
--   connectInfo</tt> is a variant of <a>waitPostgreSqlVerboseFormat</a>
--   which catches all exceptions derived from <a>SomeException</a> and
--   formats retry attempt information using <a>defaultLogMsg</a> before
--   passing the resulting <a>String</a> to <tt>out</tt>.
waitPostgreSqlVerbose :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => (String -> m ()) -> RetryPolicyM m -> ConnectInfo -> m Connection

-- | <a>waitPostgreSqlVerboseFormat</a> <tt>outputHandler retryPolicy
--   connectInfo</tt> is a variant of <a>waitPostgreSqlWith</a> which
--   installs an extra handler based on <a>logRetries</a> which passes
--   status information for each retry attempt to <tt>outputHandler</tt>.
waitPostgreSqlVerboseFormat :: forall e m. (MonadIO m, MonadMask m, Exception e) => (Bool -> e -> RetryStatus -> m ()) -> RetryPolicyM m -> ConnectInfo -> m Connection

-- | <a>waitPostgreSqlWith</a> <tt>extraHandlers retryPolicy
--   connectInfo</tt> will attempt to connect to the PostgreSQL server
--   using <tt>connectInfo</tt> and check that the server is ready to
--   accept commands. If this check fails, <tt>retryPolicy</tt> is used to
--   determine whether (and how often) this function should attempt to
--   retry establishing the connection. By default, this function will
--   retry after all exceptions (except for those given by
--   <a>skipAsyncExceptions</a>). This behaviour may be customised with
--   <tt>extraHandlers</tt> which are installed after
--   <a>skipAsyncExceptions</a>, but before the default exception handler.
--   The <tt>extraHandlers</tt> may also be used to report retry attempts
--   to e.g. the standard output or a logger.
waitPostgreSqlWith :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => [RetryStatus -> Handler m Bool] -> RetryPolicyM m -> ConnectInfo -> m Connection


-- | This module exports variants of the functions from <a>Network.Wait</a>
--   specialised for Redis servers. In addition to checking whether a
--   connection can be established, the functions in this module also check
--   whether the Redis server is ready to accept commands using
--   <a>checkedConnect</a>. Unlike <a>checkedConnect</a>, we don't give up
--   if the connection fails, but instead use the specified retry policy to
--   try again. All functions in this module return the established
--   connection if successful.
module Network.Wait.Redis

-- | <a>waitRedis</a> <tt>retryPolicy connectInfo</tt> is a variant of
--   <a>waitRedisWith</a> which does not install any additional handlers.
waitRedis :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => RetryPolicyM m -> ConnectInfo -> m Connection

-- | <a>waitRedisVerbose</a> <tt>outputHandler retryPolicy connectInfo</tt>
--   is a variant of <a>waitRedisVerboseFormat</a> which catches all
--   exceptions derived from <a>SomeException</a> and formats retry attempt
--   information using <a>defaultLogMsg</a> before passing the resulting
--   <a>String</a> to <tt>out</tt>.
waitRedisVerbose :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => (String -> m ()) -> RetryPolicyM m -> ConnectInfo -> m Connection

-- | <a>waitRedisVerboseFormat</a> <tt>outputHandler retryPolicy
--   connectInfo</tt> is a variant of <a>waitRedisWith</a> which installs
--   an extra handler based on <a>logRetries</a> which passes status
--   information for each retry attempt to <tt>outputHandler</tt>.
waitRedisVerboseFormat :: forall e m. (MonadIO m, MonadMask m, Exception e) => (Bool -> e -> RetryStatus -> m ()) -> RetryPolicyM m -> ConnectInfo -> m Connection

-- | <a>waitRedisWith</a> <tt>extraHandlers retryPolicy connectInfo</tt>
--   will attempt to connect to the Redis server using <tt>connectInfo</tt>
--   and check that the server is ready to accept commands. If this check
--   fails, <tt>retryPolicy</tt> is used to determine whether (and how
--   often) this function should attempt to retry establishing the
--   connection. By default, this function will retry after all exceptions
--   (except for those given by <a>skipAsyncExceptions</a>). This behaviour
--   may be customised with <tt>extraHandlers</tt> which are installed
--   after <a>skipAsyncExceptions</a>, but before the default exception
--   handler. The <tt>extraHandlers</tt> may also be used to report retry
--   attempts to e.g. the standard output or a logger.
waitRedisWith :: (MonadIO m, MonadMask m) => [RetryStatus -> Handler m Bool] -> RetryPolicyM m -> ConnectInfo -> m Connection
